free_text: Cadet mentioned being pushed or grabbed at home and appeared anxious about returning there after activities. Staff have agreed to monitor the situation closely and to share information with the safeguarding lead for further review. Peers have quietly expressed concern about the cadet and asked staff to check whether they are okay.

Cadet hinted that they are being pressured to go to addresses with people they do not know well and feel unable to say no. Safeguarding procedures should be followed without delay, with a clear record of actions and decisions taken. The information has been logged on the safeguarding system and will be reviewed at the next supervision meeting.

Cadet mentioned being in private online forums where extremist views are discussed and has repeated some of these views at parade. The concern appears low-level at this stage; staff will continue to observe and provide a safe space for the cadet to talk. Staff are considering whether any immediate practical support can be offered to reduce pressure on the cadet.

Another cadet reported that this cadet has spoken about wanting to travel to conflict zones and ‘fight for a cause’. There are indicators of immediate or very serious risk and urgent safeguarding action is required in line with policy. Peers have quietly expressed concern about the cadet and asked staff to check whether they are okay.

Third-party information was received suggesting there may be welfare concerns for this cadet, but further clarification is needed. No urgent safeguarding action is required currently, though the information has been recorded and will be reviewed periodically.

Cadet reported being added to group chats where explicit or disturbing content is shared, and feels pressured to remain in them. No urgent safeguarding action is required currently, though the information has been recorded and will be reviewed periodically.

Cadet disclosed that relatives have spoken about a ‘special procedure’ to be carried out when they visit family abroad and appeared very anxious about this. There are indicators of immediate or very serious risk and urgent safeguarding action is required in line with policy.

Cadet mentioned being in private online forums where extremist views are discussed and has repeated some of these views at parade. The safeguarding lead must be informed immediately and emergency procedures may need to be initiated.

No urgent safeguarding action is required currently, though the information has been recorded and will be reviewed periodically.
